conversation.content: We need a solution for senior leadership.
Happy to help narrow that down. Who is this meant for?
The pool consists of CXOs, director-level postions; people with more than 15 years of experience.

For such roles, the OPQ32r is the right instrument — it measures 32 workplace behaviour dimensions including strategic thinking, influencing style, and leadership. One question before I commit to a report format:is this for a newly created position, or developmental feedback for an executive already in role?
Selection — comparing candidates against a leadership benchmark.
For selection with a leadership benchmark, the instrument plus two relevant report formats.
Perfect, that's what we need.

persona: Senior Rust networking infrastructure engineer hiring

conversation.content: I'm hiring a senior Rust engineer for high-performance networking infrastructure. What assessments should I use?

SHL's catalog doesn't currently include a Rust-specific knowledge test. The closest fit for a senior IC is Smart Interview Live Coding — an adaptive live-coding interview where your panel can frame Rust-specific tasks directly. Linux Programming covers systems depth, and Networking and Implementation covers the infrastructure dimension. Want me to build a shortlist from these?
Yes, go ahead. Should I also add a cognitive test for this level?

Yes — Verify G+ is appropriate for senior technical candidates, covering inductive, numerical, and deductive reasoning in a single adaptive test. Full shortlist: I'm including OPQ32r by default as the personality component for a senior IC hire — say the word if you'd rather drop it.
That works. Thanks.
We're screening 500 entry-level contact centre agents. Inbound calls, customer service focus. What should we use?
Before I shape the stack — what language are the calls in? That drives which spoken-language screen we use.
English.

SVAR has four English variants in the catalog: US, UK, Australian, and Indian accent. The choice matters because the screen is calibrated for the accent your callers will hear. Which fits your operation?
US.
For high-volume entry-level contact centre screening in English US, the right stack layers a spoken language screen, a simulation, and a behavioural fit component.
Is the Contact Center Call Simulation different from the Customer Service Phone Simulation?

Yes — distinct products. The Customer Service Phone Simulation is an older bundled solution (B, P, S) combining personality, behaviour, and simulation in one package. The Contact Center Call Simulation (New) is a standalone, newer simulation focused purely on the in-call interaction. Many clients use the new simulation for volume screening and the older solution for finalist-stage depth.
Perfect — new simulation for volume, old solution for finalists. Confirmed.

relevant_assessments: Entry Level Customer Serv - Retail & Contact Center

conversation.content: Hiring graduate financial analysts — final-year students, no work experience. We need numerical reasoning and a finance knowledge test.
For graduate-level financial analysts.
Good. Can you also add a situational judgement element — work-context decision making for graduates?
Added Graduate Scenarios — SHL's situational judgement test designed specifically for graduate-level candidates. The previous three items are unchanged.
That covers it. Numerical + Graduate Scenarios as first filter, domain tests for shortlisted candidates.
As part of our restructuring and annual talent audit, we need to re-skill our Sales organization. What solutions do you recommend?

For a compact audit-and-development stack: self-reported skills (GSA plus its development report for re-skilling initiative), personality via OPQ32r, a sales-specific OPQ view with optional motivators, and Sales Transformation for how reps show up in a digital-first selling model. First-line sales managers can follow the same spine and use the Sales Manager Sales Transformation report from the catalog when you split leader vs. rep reporting.
What’s the difference between OPQ and OPQ MQ Sales Report?

OPQ (OPQ32r) is the underlying personality questionnaire: a broad, standard measure of workplace behavioural style used across roles and decisions. OPQ MQ Sales Report is a reporting product, not a different questionnaire. It summarizes OPQ results in a sales-specific way and can optionally include Motivation Questionnaire (MQ) results.
Clear. We’ll use OPQ for everyone and add MQ only where we want motivators in the Sales Report; keeping the five solutions as our audit stack.

persona: Industrial plant operator hiring for chemical facility

conversation.content: We're hiring plant operators for a chemical facility. Safety is absolute top priority — reliability, procedure compliance, never cutting corners. What do you recommend?

For a safety-critical frontline role where dependability and rule compliance are the primary concern, the assessment focus must be on personality predictors of safety behaviour — not just knowledge tests. A knowledge test tells you what someone knows about safety; a personality instrument predicts whether they'll actually follow through.
What's the difference between the DSI and the Safety & Dependability 8.0?

Both measure safety-relevant personality, but at different levels. The DSI is a standalone instrument measuring integrity, reliability, and safety attitudes — used across sectors. The Manufacturing & Industrial Safety & Dependability 8.0 is a sector-specific bundled solution with norms calibrated to manufacturing and industrial workforces. If your facility is industrial-classified, the 8.0 gives you industry norms. If you want a general standalone instrument, the DSI is the right choice.
We're industrial. The 8.0 bundle is the right fit. Confirmed.
We're hiring bilingual healthcare admin staff in South Texas — they handle patient records and need to be assessed in Spanish. HIPAA compliance is critical. What assessments work?

There's a real catalog constraint here: the role-specific knowledge tests for healthcare admin — HIPAA (Security), Medical Terminology, Microsoft Word — are English-only. The personality measures (OPQ32r, DSI) do support Latin American Spanish. Two ways to run this: (a) Hybrid: knowledge tests in English, personality in Spanish. Workable if your candidates are functionally bilingual at a working level for the English knowledge tests. (b) Personality-only in Spanish, and assess HIPAA/medical terminology through your own structured interview. Trades off measurement rigour for language fit. Which fits your candidate pool better?
They're functionally bilingual — English fluent for written work. Go with the hybrid.

For a trust-sensitive role with patient-record access, two complementary personality measures: DSI focuses specifically on dependability and safety-critical reliability — directly relevant to HIPAA-protected information handling. OPQ32r gives a broader workplace-behaviour profile. Both run in Latin American Spanish. Full hybrid battery.
Are we legally required under HIPAA to test all staff who touch patient records? And does this SHL test satisfy that requirement?

Those are legal compliance questions outside what I can advise on — I can help you select assessments, but not interpret regulatory obligations or whether a specific test satisfies a legal requirement. Your legal or compliance team is the right resource for that. What I can confirm: HIPAA (Security) measures knowledge of HIPAA security provisions. Whether using it fulfils a particular regulatory obligation is a question for counsel.
Understood. Keep the shortlist as-is.
I need to quickly screen admin assistants for Excel and Word daily.

For a quick knowledge check, the knowledge-only variants are the right fit — they test conceptual understanding. We also have end-to-end simulations to test these capabilties but I have excluded them due to the time constraint. I've also included OPQ32r as a default personality measure — admin-assistant hires benefit from a behavioural fit signal beyond pure tool knowledge. Say the word if you'd prefer to skip personality.
In that case, I am OK with adding a simulation - we want to capture the capabilties.
Understood.Here's the updated list.
That's good.

Here's the JD for an engineer we need to fill. Can you recommend an assessment battery? "Senior Full-Stack Engineer — 5+ years across Core Java, Spring, REST API design, Angular, SQL/relational databases, AWS deployment, and Docker. Will own end-to-end microservice delivery, contribute to architectural decisions, and mentor mid-level engineers. Strong CI/CD and cloud-native experience required."

That JD spans seven distinct areas — Core Java, Spring, REST APIs, Angular, SQL, AWS, and Docker. The catalog has dedicated knowledge tests for each. A focused recommendation needs to know what the candidate will actually own. To narrow this down: Is this a backend-leaning role (Java / Spring / SQL heavy) or a frontend-heavy role or a true balanced full-stack role with significant Angular work?
Backend-leaning. Day-one priorities are Core Java and Spring; SQL is constant. Angular is occasional — they'd review frontend PRs but not own features.

Understood — Java, Spring, and SQL as primary; Angular as secondary review-only. One more question before I shape the battery: is the seniority closer to a senior IC (deep backend ownership, leads design on their service) or a tech lead (sets architecture across services, reviews other engineers' work)? That changes whether we lean on a knowledge-heavy battery or add a leadership/scenarios layer.
Senior IC. They lead design on their own services but don't manage other engineers directly.
For a senior IC backend engineer with Java / Spring / SQL primary and Angular secondary, here's a first shortlist focused on what they'll actually own.
Add AWS and Docker. Drop REST — the API design signal will already come through in Spring and the live interview.
Updated — REST out, AWS and Docker in.
On Java — they'd be working on existing services, not greenfield. Is the Advanced level the right pick?

Yes. Advanced covers concurrency, JVM internals, performance tuning, and the kind of design-level Java that comes up when extending production microservices — which matches 'owns end-to-end microservice delivery'. The Entry-Level test is calibrated for graduate hires reading and modifying existing code, which undershoots a senior IC. Keeping Advanced.
Do we really need Verify G+ on top of all the technical tests? Feels redundant.

Not redundant — they measure different things. The technical tests confirm the candidate already knows the stack. Verify G+ measures general reasoning ability, which predicts how quickly they'll learn parts of the stack they haven't seen and adapt when production throws something unfamiliar at them. For a senior IC making architectural calls, that signal matters. That said, if you want a leaner shortlist, dropping Verify G+ is a valid tradeoff and the battery still stands. Your call.
Keep Verify G+. Locking it in.
We run a graduate management trainee scheme. We need a full battery — cognitive, personality, and situational judgement. All recent graduates.
For a graduate management trainee battery covering all three dimensions.
But can you remove the OPQ32r and replace it with something shorter? Candidates complain it takes too long.
OPQ32r is the most relevant solution for your need. As such, there is no shorter alternative to be used as its replacement.
Drop the OPQ. Final list: Verify G+ and Graduate Scenarios.
